frameworks.name: context-dependent decision making
frameworks.one_line: sample context gates the mapping from test odor to motor action

frameworks.name: premotor cortex circuit function
frameworks.one_line: ALM holds contextual identity to specify stimulus-response association

frameworks.name: sensory/association cortex representation
frameworks.one_line: olfactory cortex encodes sample and test odors sufficient for match/non-match

core_question: Does the premotor area ALM compute match/non-match decisions or instead carry contextual information that gates stimulus-response mapping?
key_assumptions: delayed match-to-sample requires holding sample identity across a delay
context-dependent and context-free olfactory decisions can be dissociated
transient inactivation during defined epochs can localize causal contributions
manipulations: inactivation of ALM versus orbitofrontal cortex
epoch-specific inactivation confined to before the test odor
key_findings: olfactory sensory and association cortex represent sample and test odors sufficient for match/non-match
ALM inactivation before the test odor grossly impairs task performance
orbitofrontal inactivation does not impair the task
non-context-dependent olfactory decisions remain intact under ALM inactivation
interpretations: ALM carries the sample identity as context to map test odor onto action, not a match/non-match outcome
a novel ALM layer 2 pyramidal population may mediate this contextual mapping
